top_signals.title: Gold Holds Loss as Traders Weigh Prospects for US-Iran Deal
top_signals.summary: Gold held a loss, as fresh clashes in the Persian Gulf weighed on hopes for a US-Iran peace deal and reinforced concerns that inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Police issue arrest warrant for potential mastermind in Tochigi murder case
top_signals.summary: The man fled to China after the incident in which a 69-year-old woman was killed at her home during a break-in.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Europe’s Arctic Oil Dilemma Deepens as Supply Fears Grow
top_signals.summary: A dozen financial institutions from Scandinavia have urged the European Commission to remain firm in its opposition to Arctic oil drilling even as the bloc faces physical oil shortages in weeks, according to energy experts. “The Arctic is one of the planet's most vulnerable ecosystems and home to unique wildlife .... Further oil and gas expansion would add pressure to these globally significant ecosystems, by increasing the risk of oil spills and leakages,” the lenders said in a letter organized by the Nordic Center for Sustainable…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: OpenZeppelin founder says he now considers ‘all of DeFi’ unsafe
top_signals.summary: OpenZeppelin's Manuel Aráoz said he has been privately advising friends and family to exit all DeFi positions.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
